text: The surgical team places a Chalker and sets up the operating room. Following abdominal exploration, dissection begins at the sacral promontory. The peritoneum is incised from the sacral promontory to the inferior mesenteric artery, with lymph nodes at the root of the artery being dissected for DIII excision.

The operating room setup is visible, followed by the placement of surgical instruments. The surgeon begins dissection at the sacral promontory, incising the peritoneum from this point to the inferior mesenteric artery. Lymph nodes at the root of the inferior mesenteric artery are dissected for DIII excision.

The surgeon isolates, clips, and transects the left colic artery near its origin, and ligates the origin of the left colic vein. Dissection proceeds in the embryologic plane between the mesocolon and left anterior renal fissure, continuing cephalad along the ventral aspect of the inferior mesenteric vein until reaching the inferior border of the pancreas.

The surgeon isolates, clips, and transects the left colic artery near its origin, while ligating the origin of the left colic vein. Dissection proceeds in the embryologic plane between the mesocolon and left anterior renal fissure, moving cephalad along the ventral aspect of the inferior mesenteric vein until reaching the inferior border of the pancreas.

The surgeon dissects and ligates the lymph nodes surrounding the middle colic artery. The transverse mesocolon is divided from right to left along the inferior edge of the pancreas. The dissection continues along the white line of Toldt and the splenocolic ligament, with the gastrocolic ligament being incised near the lower part of the spleen to complete the mobilization of the splenic flexure.
